title: Anzac biscuits
subtitle: Coconut, oat and syrup biscuits
excerpt: Anzac biscuits in the Australian and New Zealand household tradition: rolled oats, desiccated coconut, plain flour, golden syrup, brown sugar and butter, bound with bicarbonate of soda dissolved in boiling water. Twenty biscuits, ready in under thirty minutes.
sourceNotes: St Andrew's Cookery Book (1921, Public Domain in Australia and New Zealand): documents the canonical Anzac biscuit recipe in the form still made today — oats, coconut, flour, sugar, butter, syrup, and bicarbonate of soda. Country Women's Association cookbooks (1920s, public domain): the household standard. The name attaches the biscuit to the Australian and New Zealand Army Corps; the biscuit itself predates the name.

recipe.freezeNotes: Bake, cool, freeze in a tin for up to 3 months. They keep crisp in a sealed tin at room temperature for a week.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The mix holds in a bowl, covered, for an hour before shaping if you need to step away.

recipeTools.notes: For melting the butter and syrup.

glossaryTerms.definition: Bicarbonate of soda dissolved in boiling water and stirred into melted butter froths up like a small foam. The foamy mixture distributes the alkali evenly through the dough and, when it meets the acidic golden syrup, the resulting reaction lifts and aerates the biscuit. Add the bicarb solution to the warm syrup-and-butter, stir, and use straight away — the lift is at its strongest in the first minute.

content.text: Anzac biscuits are an Australian and New Zealand household standard from the early twentieth century: rolled oats, desiccated coconut, plain flour, brown sugar, butter, golden syrup, and bicarbonate of soda dissolved in boiling water. The dough is rolled into walnut-sized balls, flattened on a tray, and baked low until deep gold.
For a chewier biscuit, take them out at 12 minutes when the centres still look pale; for a crisp snap, bake the full 15 until the colour is even all the way to the middle.

Heat the oven to 160°C fan and line two baking trays. Mix the {{rolled-oats}}, {{plain-flour}}, {{desiccated-coconut}} and {{soft-brown-sugar}} in a wide bowl. Melt the {{unsalted-butter}} and {{golden-syrup}} in a small pan over a low heat. Stir the {{bicarbonate-of-soda}} into the boiling {{water}}; this is the

step. Stir the bicarb solution into the warm butter-and-syrup; it will foam. Pour into the dry mix and stir to a sticky, even dough. Roll into 20 walnut-sized balls and flatten each on the tray with the palm of your hand to a 6 mm disc, leaving 4 cm between them.

Bake for 12–15 minutes; the biscuits will spread a little and turn deep gold. They will be soft when they come out — that is correct. Cool 5 minutes on the tray, then transfer to a rack. Crisp on the rack as they cool.
